get.summary: Get issues within a project filterable by query parameters.
get.description:
Gets all issues within the project specified by the query parameter `projectId`, and optionally filtered by various parameters.

**Basic Filtering:**
- **Multiple IDs**: Pass several `id` values to retrieve multiple issues at once
- **POAM IDs**: Pass several `poamId` values to retrieve multiple issues (requires `projectId`)

**Content Filtering:**
- **`deviationType`**: Retrieve issues containing deviations of the specified type
- **`title`**: Retrieve issues whose title contains the provided string (case-insensitive)
- **`kev`**: Retrieve issues containing CVE IDs present in the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog
- **`cveId`**: Retrieve issues containing the specified CVE identifier (OR logic for multiple CVE IDs)
- **`asset`**: Retrieve issues with at least one of the provided asset names (case-sensitive, exact match)
- **`internetReachableVulnerability`**: Filter issues by whether they represent an internet-reachable vulnerability (boolean)
- **`likelyExploitableVulnerability`**: Filter issues by whether they represent a likely-exploitable vulnerability (boolean)

**Date Range Filtering:**

Date ranges are inclusive. You may provide one side of the date range to get an unbounded range. Date format for all filters: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., "2024-01-01")
- **Effective Date**: Use `effectiveDateStart` and `effectiveDateEnd` (filters the `createdAt` field returned by the API)
- **Due Date**: Use `dueDateStart` and `dueDateEnd`
- **Status Date**: Use `statusDateStart` and `statusDateEnd`
- **Evaluation Date**: Use `evaluationDateStart` and `evaluationDateEnd`

get.summary: Get all artifacts for the specified evidence
get.description: Retrieves all artifacts associated with a specific evidence record. Artifacts are digital files, documents, or URL references that support and validate evidence.

schema.description: An id that represents an evidence within the Paramify application.

parameters.name: id

schema.description: Filter artifacts by specific artifact IDs. Multiple IDs can be provided to fetch several artifacts in a single request.

items.description: An id that represents an artifact within the Paramify application.

parameters.description: Filter artifacts by specific artifact IDs. Multiple IDs can be provided to fetch several artifacts in a single request.

parameters.name: originalFileName

schema.description: Filter artifacts by their original file names. Multiple file names can be provided to fetch several artifacts in a single request.

parameters.description: Filter artifacts by their original file names. Multiple file names can be provided to fetch several artifacts in a single request.

parameters.name: createdAt

schema.description: Filter artifacts created on specific dates. Use this to find artifacts that were uploaded or created on particular dates. Date format should be YYYY-MM-DD. Multiple dates can be provided.

parameters.description: Filter artifacts created on specific dates. Use this to find artifacts that were uploaded or created on particular dates. Date format should be YYYY-MM-DD. Multiple dates can be provided.

parameters.name: effectiveDate

schema.description: Filter artifacts by their effective dates. The effective date represents when the artifact data was actually valid or captured, which may differ from the upload date. Multiple dates can be provided.

parameters.description: Filter artifacts by their effective dates. The effective date represents when the artifact data was actually valid or captured, which may differ from the upload date. Multiple dates can be provided.

parameters.name: isUrl

schema.description: Filter artifacts by type - whether they are URL references (true) or uploaded files (false). Use true to get only URL artifacts, false to get only file uploads.

parameters.description: Filter artifacts by type - whether they are URL references (true) or uploaded files (false). Use true to get only URL artifacts, false to get only file uploads.

id.description: Unique identifier for the artifact

post.summary: Upload a new file artifact for the specified evidence
post.description: Uploads a file as an artifact and associates it with the specified evidence record. The uploaded file will be securely stored and made available for future reference. Maximum file size: 10 GB.

schema.description: An id that represents an evidence within the Paramify application.

file.description:
The file to be uploaded as an artifact. Any file type is supported. The original filename will be preserved and used as the default title if no title is specified in the metadata.

**IMPORTANT**: Filenames containing certain special characters may cause upload errors. URL-encode your filename to avoid issues -- it will be decoded automatically.

title.description: A descriptive title for the artifact. If not provided, the original filename will be used as the title.

note.description: Additional notes or comments about the artifact. Use this field to provide context, explanations, or any relevant information about the artifact.
note.example: This report contains the results of the quarterly vulnerability scan conducted on our production infrastructure.

effectiveDate.description: The date when this artifact was effective or relevant. This may differ from the upload date and represents when the data or document was actually valid. If not provided, defaults to the current date.

channelId.description: The id of the channel that owns the artifact

artifact.description: Schema for creating new file artifacts. All fields are optional - you can upload a file with minimal metadata and enhance it later.

id.description: Unique identifier for the artifact

title.description: Display title of the artifact. For file uploads, this defaults to the original filename if not provided during creation.

originalFileName.description: The original filename when the artifact was uploaded. This field is null for URL artifacts.

note.description: Additional notes or comments about the artifact. Can be null if no note was provided.
note.example: This report contains the results of the quarterly vulnerability scan conducted on our production infrastructure.

fileType.description: MIME type of the file (e.g., application/pdf, image/png). This field is null for URL artifacts.

createdBy.description: Information about the user who created the artifact

effectiveDate.description: The date when this artifact was effective or relevant. This may differ from createdAt and represents when the data was actually valid.

validators.description: Information about the validators that have run or are running on the artifact

post.summary: Submit intake data for an assessment
post.description:
## Overview
Uploads a CSV, XML, JSON, or Nessus file as an artifact and attaches it to the intake on a vulnerability or configuration assessment specified by the `assessmentId`. Only CSV, XML, JSON, and Nessus files are supported.

Maximum file size: 10 GB.

### Effective Date Handling
The effective date specified will be used to determine what cycle(s) to upload the artifact to. If no effective date is specified, the current date will be used as the effective date.

### Cycle Creation and Management
The artifact will be added to the intake for each cycle on the assessment whose start and end date overlap the artifact's effective date. If no such cycle can be found, a new one will be created. The created cycle's start date will be the beginning of the effective date's month. The cycle's end date will be calculated based on the assessment's frequency.

### Evidence Handling
If the intake on the cycle does not have an attached evidence, the evidence from the previous cycle's intake will be used. If there is no previous cycle, a new evidence will be created.

### Multiple Cycle Behavior
If multiple cycles fall within the range of the artifact's effective date, the artifact will be attached to each cycle's intake. If the multiple cycle intakes have different evidences, one artifact will be created for each evidence.

### Existing Artifacts
If any of the cycle intakes already have artifacts attached, the new artifact will be added without removing existing artifacts.

schema.description: An id that represents an assessment within the Paramify application.

file.description:
The file to be uploaded as an artifact. Any file type is supported. The original filename will be preserved and used as the default title if no title is specified in the metadata.

**IMPORTANT**: Filenames containing certain special characters may cause upload errors. URL-encode your filename to avoid issues -- it will be decoded automatically.

title.description: A descriptive title for the artifact. If not provided, the original filename will be used as the title.

note.description: Additional notes or comments about the artifact. Use this field to provide context, explanations, or any relevant information about the artifact.
note.example: This report contains the results of the quarterly vulnerability scan conducted on our production infrastructure.

effectiveDate.description: The date when this artifact was effective or relevant. This may differ from the upload date and represents when the data or document was actually valid. If not provided, defaults to the current date.

channelId.description: The id of the channel that owns the artifact

artifact.description: Schema for creating new file artifacts. All fields are optional - you can upload a file with minimal metadata and enhance it later.

id.description: Unique identifier for the artifact

title.description: Display title of the artifact. For file uploads, this defaults to the original filename if not provided during creation.

originalFileName.description: The original filename when the artifact was uploaded. This field is null for URL artifacts.

pathname.description: For file artifacts: the direct URL to retrieve the stored file. This link may expire after about 60 minutes; request the artifact again to get a new link. For URL artifacts: this is the full external URL to the resource.

note.description: Additional notes or comments about the artifact. Can be null if no note was provided.
note.example: This report contains the results of the quarterly vulnerability scan conducted on our production infrastructure.

isUrl.description: Indicates the artifact type: true for URL artifacts, false for uploaded file artifacts

fileType.description: MIME type of the file (e.g., application/pdf, image/png). This field is null for URL artifacts.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the artifact was created in the system

createdBy.description: Information about the user who created the artifact

effectiveDate.description: The date when this artifact was effective or relevant. This may differ from createdAt and represents when the data was actually valid.

validators.description: Information about the validators that have run or are running on the artifact

items.description: Complete artifact object containing all metadata, file information, and creation details. Artifacts can be either uploaded files or URL references.

schema.description: An array of newly created artifacts. Typically only one artifact will be created, unless multiple assessment cycles overlap the effective date and have different evidences attached to their intakes. At least one artifact will always be created if the request was successful.

post.summary: Attach an existing CSV, XML, JSON, or Nessus file artifact to an assessment cycle's intake
post.description:
## Overview
Attaches an existing CSV, XML, JSON, or Nessus file artifact to the intake of the assessment cycle specified by the `assessmentId` and `cycleId`. Only file artifacts with a CSV, XML, JSON, or Nessus file will be accepted. The specified cycle must be a cycle on the specified assessment.

### Evidence Handling
If the cycle's intake does not have an attached evidence, the evidence the artifact is attached to will automatically be attached to the intake. If the evidence attached to the cycle's intake is different from the evidence that the artifact is on, an error will be thrown.

### Existing Artifacts
If the cycle's intake already has artifacts attached, the new artifact will be added without removing existing artifacts.

schema.description: An id that represents an assessment within the Paramify application.
An id that represents an assessment cycle within the Paramify application.

artifactId.description: The internal uuid of the CSV file artifact to attach to the assessment cycle's intake.

id.description: Unique identifier for the artifact

title.description: Display title of the artifact. For file uploads, this defaults to the original filename if not provided during creation.

originalFileName.description: The original filename when the artifact was uploaded. This field is null for URL artifacts.

pathname.description: For file artifacts: the direct URL to retrieve the stored file. This link may expire after about 60 minutes; request the artifact again to get a new link. For URL artifacts: this is the full external URL to the resource.

note.description: Additional notes or comments about the artifact. Can be null if no note was provided.
note.example: This report contains the results of the quarterly vulnerability scan conducted on our production infrastructure.

isUrl.description: Indicates the artifact type: true for URL artifacts, false for uploaded file artifacts

fileType.description: MIME type of the file (e.g., application/pdf, image/png). This field is null for URL artifacts.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the artifact was created in the system

createdBy.description: Information about the user who created the artifact

effectiveDate.description: The date when this artifact was effective or relevant. This may differ from createdAt and represents when the data was actually valid.

validators.description: Information about the validators that have run or are running on the artifact

schema.description: The artifact that was attached to the assessment cycle's intake.

get.description: Returns all validators in the workspace. Each validator is either an AUTOMATED validator (applies a regex to artifact content and evaluates the result with validation rules) or an ATTESTATION validator (presents yes/no questions to a reviewer, optionally with nested follow-up rules).

parameters.name: ids

schema.description: Filter by validator ID. Multiple IDs can be provided.

parameters.description: Filter by validator ID. Multiple IDs can be provided.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by validator type. AUTOMATED or ATTESTATION.

parameters.description: Filter by validator type. AUTOMATED or ATTESTATION.

id.description: A UUID that uniquely identifies this validator within Paramify.

name.description: The display name of the validator.

statement.description: A human-readable description of what this validator checks.

type.description: AUTOMATED validators apply a regex to artifact content and evaluate the result using one or more validation rules.

regex.description: The regular expression applied to each incoming artifact.

regexOperation.description: Describes how the regex match result is extracted before being compared. MATCH_COUNT counts the total number of matches; MATCH_GROUP extracts the content of a specific capture group.

criteria.description: The comparison operator applied between the extracted regex value and the target value. One of: CONTAINS, DOES_NOT_CONTAIN, ENDS_WITH, DOES_NOT_END_WITH, EQUALS, NOT_EQUALS, GREATER_THAN, GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO, LESS_THAN, LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO, STARTS_WITH, DOES_NOT_START_WITH.

value.description: The right-hand side of the criteria comparison. MATCH_COUNT uses the match count, MATCH_GROUP uses a specific capture group, CUSTOM_TEXT uses a literal string.

validationRules.description: Ordered list of rules that evaluate the regex result. Rules are evaluated in index order.

regex.description: The regular expression applied to each incoming artifact.

regexOperation.description: Describes how the regex match result is extracted before being compared. MATCH_COUNT counts the total number of matches; MATCH_GROUP extracts the content of a specific capture group.

criteria.description: The comparison operator applied between the extracted regex value and the target value. One of: CONTAINS, DOES_NOT_CONTAIN, ENDS_WITH, DOES_NOT_END_WITH, EQUALS, NOT_EQUALS, GREATER_THAN, GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO, LESS_THAN, LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL_TO, STARTS_WITH, DOES_NOT_START_WITH.

value.description: The right-hand side of the criteria comparison. MATCH_COUNT uses the match count, MATCH_GROUP uses a specific capture group, CUSTOM_TEXT uses a literal string.
